label: Hidden Markov models & CRFs

node.label: Hidden Markov models & CRFs
node.definition: Hidden Markov models generate sequences through latent Markov state, whereas conditional random fields directly model structured output distributions conditioned on observations.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Hidden Markov models & CRFs is to connect Sequences, events & time series, Latent variables & hidden state to downstream capabilities including Time-series forecasting, anomaly & fraud detection, Document intelligence & information extraction.
examples: substrate for Time-series forecasting, anomaly & fraud detection
substrate for Document intelligence & information extraction
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Sequences, events & time series, Latent variables & hidden state.

dependencies.rationale: Hidden Markov models & CRFs consumes, emits, or organizes information using Sequences, events & time series.
Hidden Markov models & CRFs consumes, emits, or organizes information using Latent variables & hidden state.
Hidden Markov models & CRFs is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family MCMC, EM & probabilistic message passing.
Hidden Markov models & CRFs is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Maximum likelihood & cross-entropy.
